content:
Three modern JavaScript techniques that quietly eliminated bugs, simplified asynchronous code, and made my applications feel faster without writing more code.

3 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

If the first three techniques helped me write cleaner code, these next three completely changed how I think about solving problems.
A few years ago, whenever I started a new feature, my first instinct was to write more code.
Need to remove duplicates?
Write a loop.
Need to wait for multiple API calls?
Write nested promises.
Need to safely access object properties?
Add five if statements just to avoid a runtime error.
Looking back, none of those solutions were terrible.
They were just unnecessary.
Modern JavaScript has evolved a lot, and some of its most useful features are also the most overlooked.
For Part 1: CLICK HERE
Use Set Whenever You Need Unique Values
For the longest time, I removed duplicate values manually.
